narration: Your cells run on tiny ancient power plants — and they have their own DNA.
visual_description: Hook visual: Your cells run on tiny ancient power plants — and they have their own DNA.
ai_image_prompt: Hook visual: Your cells run on tiny ancient power plants — and they have their own DNA.
ai_video_prompt: Cinematic motion: Hook visual: Your cells run on tiny ancient power plants — and they have their own DNA.

narration: Meet mitochondria, the energy factories that convert food into fuel, powering every move you make.
visual_description: Context visual: Meet mitochondria, the energy factories that convert food into fuel, powering every move you make.
ai_image_prompt: Context visual: Meet mitochondria, the energy factories that convert food into fuel, powering every move you make.
ai_video_prompt: Cinematic motion: Context visual: Meet mitochondria, the energy factories that convert food into fuel, powering every move you make.

narration: But here’s the twist: They were once free-living bacteria. Yes, you heard that right!
visual_description: Escalation visual: But here’s the twist: They were once free-living bacteria. Yes, you heard that right!
ai_image_prompt: Escalation visual: But here’s the twist: They were once free-living bacteria. Yes, you heard that right!
ai_video_prompt: Cinematic motion: Escalation visual: But here’s the twist: They were once free-living bacteria. Yes, you heard that right!

narration: This endosymbiotic theory explains how they became essential to our cells, merging with us over billions of years.
visual_description: Evidence visual: This endosymbiotic theory explains how they became essential to our cells, merging with us over billions of years.
ai_image_prompt: Evidence visual: This endosymbiotic theory explains how they became essential to our cells, merging with us over billions of years.
ai_video_prompt: Cinematic motion: Evidence visual: This endosymbiotic theory explains how they became essential to our cells, merging with us over billions of years.

narration: So next time you feel energized, remember — it’s those ancient power plants working hard for you.
